title: TimeSeriesForecastingInput
description: Inputs for Time Series Forecasting inference. Each input series lies on a regular grid defined by its `start` and `parameters.frequency`. Missing observations are encoded as `null` inside `target`; clients with irregular data should resample before calling.

inputs.description: One or more historical time series to forecast. Output order matches input order.

target.description: Historical values on a regular grid. Always a 2D array of shape [num_timesteps][num_channels]. Univariate series use a length-1 channel axis. Missing observations are encoded as `null`.

start.description: Optional ISO-8601 timestamp of the first observation. Combined with `parameters.frequency` it defines the time index for both the input and the returned forecast timestamps. If omitted, observations are treated as an integer sequence and the response has no `timestamps`.

past_covariates.description: Optional named covariates observed over the historical window. Each key maps to a 1D array aligned 1:1 with the time axis of `target`. Missing values are encoded as `null`. Models that don't support covariates silently ignore this field.

future_covariates.description: Optional named covariates known over the forecast horizon. Each key maps to a 1D array of length `parameters.prediction_length`.

static_covariates.description: Optional static (time-invariant) numeric features for the series.

metadata.description: Opaque per-series metadata. Echoed verbatim in the corresponding output item. Not consumed by any model. Maximum 4KB per item.

prediction_length.description: Number of future timesteps to forecast. Defaults to the model's native horizon.

frequency.description: Optional pandas offset alias defining the grid spacing. Common values: 's' (second), 'min' (minute), 'h' (hour), 'D' (day), 'B' (business day), 'W' (week), 'ME' / 'MS' (month end/start), 'QE' / 'QS' (quarter), 'YE' / 'YS' (year). Accepts multipliers and anchors (e.g. '15min', 'W-MON'). When provided alongside a per-item `start`, the response includes `timestamps`. Servers reject aliases they don't support.

quantile_levels.description: Quantile levels in (0, 1) to return alongside the mean. Omit to receive only the mean.

num_samples.description: For sample-based probabilistic models, the number of sample paths to draw. Ignored by models that don't produce samples. May be combined with `quantile_levels`.
